QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_20

question.text: As per the SEBI circular effective May 2025, equity derivatives contracts on the National Stock Exchange (NSE) expire on:

answer.text: The last Tuesday of the expiry month
The last Thursday of the expiry month
The last Friday of the expiry month
The last Wednesday of the expiry month

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** As per the SEBI circular issued in May 2025, NSE equity derivatives contracts (including index and stock futures) expire on the LAST TUESDAY of the expiry month. BSE equity derivatives contracts expire on the LAST THURSDAY. If the last Tuesday/Thursday is a trading holiday, the contract expires on the preceding trading day.
**The Trap:** Previously, NSE expiry was on Thursday. The change to Tuesday for NSE (per May 2025 circular) is a recent regulatory update that candidates must know.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is a regulatory topic tested in Series VIII. Know both NSE (Tuesday) and BSE (Thursday) expiry days.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NSE expiry = Last Tuesday. BSE expiry = Last Thursday. Holiday = previous trading day.
